T0130.title: Pig Hunting
T0130.definition: Pig hunting is the opening stage of a fraud chain, referring to the act of screening and locking in potential victims.
T0130.description: In pig-butchering scams or schemes targeting specific demographics, pig hunting is the critical front-end traffic generation step. Scammers use social media, matchmaking platforms, telemarketing, or fake job ads to batch-screen target groups who are emotionally vulnerable, have weak defenses, or are desperate for money. This stage focuses on collecting basic personal data and contact details, supplying a precise list of leads for the subsequent pig-farming stage. The efficiency of pig hunting directly determines the crew’s overall output and is typically handled by dedicated traffic or data teams.
T0130.usageExample: This week the pig-hunting team cast their net across several dating apps and pulled in over thirty divorced single parents; all the contact info has already been handed over to the pig-farming crew.
